region.description: The original problem as posed — often copied from a textbook or written by an instructor. May begin with directive language ("Graph the function", "Find the inverse", "Solve for x").
region.indicators: Directive verbs, standalone equations without solution steps, different handwriting or ink color from solution work.

region.description: A formal or informal mathematical definition, often introduced with "Definition:" or descriptive language explaining a concept.
region.indicators: Declarative statements about mathematical objects, often in a distinct color or with underlines.

region.description: A stated mathematical property, theorem, or rule being applied (e.g., "The sum of the measures of the angles of any triangle is 180°").
region.indicators: Universal statements, often highlighted or in a distinct color, stated without proof.

region.description: A complete worked solution including setup, intermediate steps, and final answer. The core unit of mathematical instruction.
region.indicators: Labeled with "Example" or numbered, contains a problem followed by step-by-step solution.

region.description: The step-by-step algebraic or logical progression within a worked example. Each step should follow logically from the previous one.
region.indicators: Sequential equations or expressions connected by logical operations, aligned vertically or following left-to-right progression.

region.description: A final answer that has been visually emphasized — boxed, circled, underlined, or written in a distinct color — to indicate it is the conclusion of a solution.
region.indicators: Rectangular border, circle, color change, or other visual emphasis around a final expression.

region.description: Explanatory text alongside mathematical work — verbal descriptions of steps, reminders, or clarifying notes.
region.indicators: Natural language text interspersed with or adjacent to mathematical expressions, often in a different color.

region.description: A title or heading indicating the topic or section of the notes.
region.indicators: Larger text, distinct color, underlined, positioned at top of page or before new content blocks.

region.description: Informal or incomplete calculations not part of the primary solution — side computations, trial values, or exploration.
region.indicators: Lighter writing, crossed-out content, marginal placement, no clear connection to main solution flow.

expression_types.type: A statement of equality: left-hand side = right-hand side.
Verbal or symbolic description of a function transformation (shift, reflect, scale).
A ratio equality: a/b = c/d, often used in similar triangle problems.
An angle measurement, typically in degrees, using the ∠ symbol.
Statements using ≅ (congruent) or ~ (similar) between geometric objects.
Domain, range, or set definitions using interval or set-builder notation.

type.name: coordinate_plane
type.description: An x-y axis system with plotted points, curves, or regions.
type.labeled_elements: axis labels, scale markings, plotted points with coordinates, curve equations, intercepts

type.name: triangle
type.description: A three-sided polygon, often with labeled vertices, side lengths, angle measures, and congruence/similarity marks.
type.labeled_elements: vertex labels (A, B, C), side lengths, angle measures (degrees), tick marks (equal sides), arc marks (equal angles), altitude/median lines

type.name: function_graph
type.description: The graph of a mathematical function on a coordinate plane, showing shape, key points, and transformations.
type.labeled_elements: function equation label, vertex/extrema coordinates, intercepts, asymptotes, transformation annotations

type.name: circle
type.description: A circle with potential labels for center, radius, diameter, chords, arcs, or inscribed/circumscribed elements.
type.labeled_elements: center point, radius length, diameter, arc measures, inscribed angles, tangent lines

type.name: parallel_lines
type.description: Lines marked as parallel (often with arrow marks), typically cut by a transversal showing angle relationships.
type.labeled_elements: parallel marks, transversal, angle measures, corresponding/alternate angle labels

type.name: generic_polygon
type.description: Any polygon (quadrilateral, pentagon, etc.) with labeled properties.
type.labeled_elements: vertex labels, side lengths, angle measures, diagonal lengths, special property marks

type.name: number_line
type.description: A one-dimensional representation showing points, intervals, or inequalities.
type.labeled_elements: scale markings, labeled points, open/closed circles, shaded regions

color.name: black_pencil
color.typical_role: Base content — figures, diagrams, primary problem setup. Often the first layer written.

color.name: blue
color.typical_role: Solution work, annotations, step-by-step derivations. The most common working ink color.

color.name: red
color.typical_role: Emphasis, boxed answers, corrections, or important results. Draws attention to final answers or key errors.

color.name: green
color.typical_role: Section headers, topic labels, or secondary annotations. Often used for organizational structure.

color.name: purple
color.typical_role: Alternative annotations or tertiary emphasis. Less common, context-dependent.

color_semantics.note: Color roles are conventions, not rules. Actual semantic roles must be inferred from context on each page. Report observed colors and their apparent roles.

pair.context: Variable 'x' appears in algebraic expressions; multiplication '×' appears between numbers or is implied.
Lowercase 'l' in labels; digit '1' in numbers; absolute value bars '|' around expressions.
Letter 'o' in labels; digit '0' in numbers; degree symbol '°' after angle measures.
Digit '2' in numbers/exponents; variable 'z' in expressions. Check position and context.
Parentheses for grouping or function arguments; brackets for intervals or matrices.
Minus sign, dash, equals. Distinguished by length, position, and context.
Angle symbol before vertex labels; less-than in inequalities; letter L in labels.
Tilde for similarity (~); similar-to (∼); approximately equal (≈). Context determines meaning.
Prime notation f'(x); superscript -1 for inverse f⁻¹(x); exponent. Check function context.
Square root radical; letter 'v' in labels; logical or. Radical has a vinculum (overbar).
